author: codex-agent

patch.value: Feature converts ad-hoc governance scripts into supported command flows: scan -> report -> optional apply, with explicit scope and safety controls.
Add governance normalize command with dry-run and apply modes
Implement a first-class pm command that detects low-signal metadata patterns and optionally applies deterministic normalization updates with auditable mutation plans.

A governance normalize command can (1) scan and report placeholder/low-signal metadata patterns, (2) emit deterministic mutation previews, and (3) apply approved updates while preserving history and ownership semantics.
Pattern catalog and replacement policies are captured from completed governance sweeps.
Reduces manual command volume and inconsistent cleanup outcomes
Improves repeatability and lowers operational error risk
Agents can run governance normalization from a supported pm command
Recent sweep required >400 scripted metadata mutations that should be productized.
Attempt full metadata normalization on tracker without custom scripts and observe missing command support.
Maintainers and agents can perform consistent tracker normalization with less manual overhead.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search 'automated standards alignment metadata normalization command' --limit 20; pm search 'bulk governance normalization command pm tracker' --limit 20; pm list-open --limit 20. Net-new because existing items capture manual execution history,not a dedicated normalize command surface.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Design should support audit-only mode first,then explicit apply mode with deterministic ordering and change summaries.

message: Create feature for governance normalize command
